arts.ttl: Nuno Espirito Santo drops four-word team news hint ahead of Arsenal vs Nottingham Forest
Mikel Arteta guaranteed to break Arsenal all-time winning record in Nottingham Forest clash
arts.sum: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta will set a new club record when he takes charge of his 250th game as manager in Saturday's Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest.

arts.ttl: Arsenal on course to blow Man United out of the water as Kieran Maguire issues £155m prediction
arts.sum: Arsenal’s renaissance on the pitch in recent years has stopped short of delivering the Premier League title but has also yielded a major financial turnaround....

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta pinpoints biggest Arsenal achievement after 250 games amid trophy drought question
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has pinpointed his biggest achievement as Arsenal manager, with the former midfielder about to rack up his 250th game as Gunners boss. "What we transmit as a football club, as a team and the people that I work with.

arts.ttl: Italy defensive options bolstered after latest Arsenal and Atalanta updates
arts.sum: Luciano Spalletti has received an enormous double-boost on Friday, as he will likely have both...

As we look to rediscover our early-season form, we can lean on the fact that we have lost only two of our 14 Premier League games against Forest, and have never on home turf. Raheem Sterling has scored four times in his four Premier League games against Forest, with his average of a goal every 68 minutes against them his best against any opponent he’s faced more than once.

arts.ttl: Calafiori’s Arsenal return confirmed: ‘Will be in the squad’ for Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Italy star Riccardo Calafiori is closing in on his return to the pitch for Arsenal...

arts.ttl: Arsenal can finally replace Gabriel Martinelli with Edu Gaspar parting transfer gift
arts.sum: Star player Bukayo Saka has scored only four goals this season and Gabriel Martinelli has scored one less, despite playing two more games. Despite spending big in the last three years, the Gunners are yet to get their hands on the Premier League title.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta makes Arsenal January transfer window admission after Stan Kroenke meeting
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta has said it was "really positive" spending time with Arsenal owners Stan and Josh Kroenke over the international break, with January transfer window plans one of the many topics discussed during November's annual meeting. The Spaniard made the trip to America following the Gunners' 1-1 draw with Chelsea on November 10, with the 42-year-old meeting with the Kroenkes to discuss the current season, future transfer plans and who could replace Edu as sporting director.

arts.ttl: Injury updates on nine Gunners ahead of Forest
arts.sum: The international break provided us with a fortnight to try and get a number of our squad fit for tomorrow’s game against Nottingham Forest, and Mikel Arteta used his pre-match press conference to provide an update on eight players. “They have had their first partial session today, so very good as well,” Mikel added.
